text.title: Mock at Boundaries
text.summary: Where to mock, partial vs full mocking, time mocking, external API levels, and the mock decision tree.

section.title: Mock at Boundaries Principle
section.rule: Mock at the architectural boundary, not inside the system under test.
Boundaries: I/O (filesystem, network), time (datetime.now), randomness (uuid4), external services.
Internal collaborators within your own codebase should use real implementations in unit tests.

example.description: Mock the HTTP client boundary, not internal service logic
example.code:
# Good: mock at the HTTP boundary def test_payment_service_retries_on_timeout(mocker): mock_post = mocker.patch("myapp.adapters.stripe.requests.post") mock_post.side_effect = [Timeout(), Timeout(), Mock(status_code=200)]
result = PaymentService().charge(amount=100)
assert result.success is True assert mock_post.call_count == 3

section.title: Partial vs Full Mocking
section.rule: Full mock: replace the entire object with Mock(). Use when you want to verify call patterns.
Partial mock: patch one method on a real object with mocker.patch.object(). Use when most behavior should remain real.

example.code: # Partial mock: only patch the external call def test_user_service_sends_welcome_email(mocker): mocker.patch.object(EmailClient, "send", return_value=True) service = UserService(email_client=EmailClient()) service.register(email="new@example.com") EmailClient.send.assert_called_once()

section.title: Time Mocking
section.rule: Never test time-dependent code against real datetime.now() — use freezegun or mocker.patch.

section.title: Decision Tree
section.rule: Ask: Does removing this dependency make the test faster or more reliable? If yes, mock it.
Ask: Is this a third-party service that may be unavailable in CI? If yes, mock it.
Ask: Is this a collaborator in my own codebase? If yes, prefer a real or fake implementation.
Ask: Am I mocking to avoid writing a difficult test? If yes, fix the design instead.
